webservice.description: Returns the authenticated user's active OAuth3 scopes with names, descriptions, granted status, and expiry timestamps
webservice.auth: Bearer token required — returns 401 if missing or invalid

outputs.total: integer — total count of scopes in response

guarantees: Returns 401 if Authorization header is missing
Returns 401 if Bearer token is invalid or expired
Scopes are time-bounded: expires_at is never null for granted scopes
Each scope entry has: name (string), description (string), granted (boolean), expires_at (ISO 8601)
owasp_guarantees: No token → 401 (not 200 with empty or another user's scopes)
User A's token cannot enumerate User B's granted scopes

frontend_dependencies: web/settings.html → OAuth3 section (active scopes list, revoke buttons)
evidence_notes: Paper 02 (OAuth3): time-bounded, revocable, auditable tokens. James Bach: what can an agent actually do? The scopes list IS the contract. expires_at is load-bearing — a scope without expiry is not OAuth3.
